title: Chipmaker preannouncement rumor before earnings
claim_to_evaluate: Several posts claimed a large chipmaker was about to preannounce a sharp revenue miss before earnings. How reliable is that call?
baseline_goal: Train the workflow to separate earnings rumor flow, selective company signals, and exchange-filed confirmation.

sources.name: Sector Blog
sources.support: The blog cited channel checks and said a miss warning was imminent.

sources.name: Company IR Calendar
sources.support: The company kept its scheduled earnings date and made no filing about changed guidance.

sources.name: CFO Conference Appearance
sources.support: The CFO said end-market demand remained uneven but did not preannounce revised revenue.

key_claims.claim: Market participants were discussing a possible preannouncement.
The company had already filed a revenue warning.
The right framing is elevated rumor risk but not confirmed guidance change.

credibility_reference.expected_judgment: Moderate-to-low confidence. Enough smoke to monitor, not enough to state a warning is coming.

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback if the output claims a guidance reset without exchange or company confirmation.

notes.winner_pattern_hint: Differentiate chatter, company commentary, and formal filing status.
notes.loser_pattern_hint: Turning channel-check rumor into a definitive earnings event.
